name: Puebla de Don Rodrigo and Camino Natural del Guadiana Loop
name_original: Dehesas de la Puebla de Don Rodrigo — circular por la Sierra de Saceruela
description: Hike the moderate 6.6-mile Puebla de Don Rodrigo and Camino Natural del Guadiana Loop, exploring 'Wild West' landscapes and the Estrecho de
region_intro: Hiking around Montes Norte offers diverse landscapes, primarily characterized by Mediterranean forests, holm oak and cork oak pastures, and winding sandy trails. The region features gentle hills, granite plateaus with dehesas, and dense Mediterranean scrub. Several rivers and streams crisscross the area, contributing to varied natural features suitable for hiking. Best hiking trails around Montes Norte The most popular hiking route is Route of La Viñuela and of the Cañada Real – Parque Nacional de Cabañeros, a 8.0 miles (12.9 km) moderate trail that takes 3 hours 31 minutes to complete. This route explores the natural features within the Parque Nacional de Cabañeros. Another top favourite among local hikers is La Viñuela Trail, a moderate 7.5 miles (12.1 km) path. This trail offers views of the surrounding Mediterranean landscape and takes approximately 3 hours 18 minutes. Local hikers also love the Boquerón del Estena Trail, a 2.7 miles (4.4 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 1 hour 11 minutes. Hiking around Montes Norte is defined by Mediterranean forests, dehesas, and gentle hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging moderate routes. The routes in Montes Norte are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 50 reviews. More than 300 hikers have used komoot to explore Montes Norte's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Montes Norte?
A: Montes Norte offers a wide variety of hiking experiences with over 40 routes available. These range from easy strolls to more challenging moderate trails, catering to different fitness levels.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Montes Norte?
A: Yes, Montes Norte has several easy trails perfect for beginners or families. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018237349/boqueron-del-estena-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Boquerón del Estena Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a shorter, easy route through varied terrain, taking about an hour and 11 minutes. Another accessible option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1094013256/rio-bullaque-circular-por-tabla-de-la-yedra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Bullaque – circular por Tabla de la Yedra&lt;/a&gt;, an easy circular path.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Montes Norte?
A: Absolutely. Montes Norte features several circular routes, which are great for experiencing diverse landscapes without retracing your steps. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1187981214/path-of-the-plaza-de-los-moros-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Path of the Plaza de los Moros — circular&lt;/a&gt;, an easy trail that offers a complete loop experience.

Q: Are there any waterfalls to see while hiking in Montes Norte?
A: Yes, the region offers beautiful natural features, including waterfalls. You can explore the area around &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5634889" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Chorrera de Horcajo Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, which is a notable highlight. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1206276399/la-chorrera-de-horcajo-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Chorrera de Horcajo Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route that can lead you to this picturesque spot.

Q: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see on the trails in Montes Norte?
A: Montes Norte is renowned for its rich biodiversity, especially in areas like the Sierra de Andújar and Sierra de Cardeña y Montoro Natural Parks. Hikers might spot the endangered Iberian lynx, Iberian wolves, and various raptors such as imperial and booted eagles. The dehesas are home to herds of pigs and grazing cows, and you might also encounter deer and wild boars.

Q: What are some interesting natural landmarks or attractions to visit near the hiking trails?
A: Beyond the trails themselves, Montes Norte boasts several unique natural landmarks. You can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4413243" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Estena Towers&lt;/a&gt; or discover the fascinating &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5636470" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Giant Marine Worm Tracks&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4413244" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Boquerón de Estena Trail&lt;/a&gt; is also considered a highlight in itself.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Montes Norte?
A: The hiking routes in Montes Norte are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 50 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse Mediterranean forests, the tranquil dehesas, and the gentle hills that define the region's natural beauty. Many appreciate the variety of options available for different ability levels.

Q: Is Montes Norte a good destination for birdwatching?
A: Yes, Montes Norte, particularly the Sierra de Cardeña y Montoro Natural Park, is an excellent area for birdwatching. It's a significant habitat for numerous raptors like imperial, royal, booted, and short-toed eagles, as well as black and griffon vultures, and black and white storks. The Sierra Norte in Mexico is also a convergence point for major bird migration routes.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Montes Norte?
A: The best time to hike in Montes Norte generally depends on the specific region. For the Spanish parks, spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and lush landscapes. In the Sierra Norte of Oaxaca, Mexico, the dry season (roughly November to May) is often preferred for hiking, avoiding the heavier rains of the wet season while still enjoying the diverse flora.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging moderate hikes in Montes Norte?
A: For those seeking a longer, moderate challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1206010020/la-vinuela-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Viñuela Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a great option, covering about 7.5 miles (12.1 km) and taking around 3 hours 18 minutes. Another substantial moderate route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1094152655/sierra-de-malagon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Malagón&lt;/a&gt;, which includes more significant elevation gain.

Q: Can I find trails that explore the Parque Nacional de Cabañeros within Montes Norte?
A: Yes, one of the most popular hiking routes in the region, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1087302914/route-of-la-vinuela-and-of-the-canada-real-parque-nacional-de-cabaneros" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of La Viñuela and of the Cañada Real – Parque Nacional de Cabañeros&lt;/a&gt;, specifically explores the natural features within this national park. It's a moderate 8.0-mile (12.9 km) trail.

Q: Are there trails that offer panoramic views of the surrounding landscape?
A: Many trails in Montes Norte provide excellent panoramic views. In the Sierra de Cardeña y Montoro Natural Park, routes often offer vistas of the Guadalquivir valley to the south and the southern face of Sierra Morena to the north. The Sierra Norte in Oaxaca, Mexico, is also known for its panoramic lookouts from its high-country trails.
tips: There are beautiful pastures on these slopes of the mountains near the town of Puebla de Don Rodrigo. Beautiful to walk there!

Municipality located in the valley of the Tierteafuera river. In the middle of the Guadiana basin, framed and partially divided by the Grande and Chico streams, two kilometers from the course of the Guadiana River.
There is evidence of habitation in this area since pre-Roman times, as demonstrated by the presence of several castles, such as the Castle or Castilnegro, and even a motilla found in the 1970s and located at the exit of the town. .

faqs.question: What is the overall difficulty of the Puebla de Don Rodrigo and Camino Natural del Guadiana Loop?
faqs.answer: This loop is rated as moderate. While some sections of the broader Camino Natural del Guadiana can be quite challenging with steep slopes and abundant vegetation, this specific 10.5 km loop offers a balanced experience. It's suitable for hikers with a reasonable level of fitness.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this hike?
faqs.answer: You'll experience a varied landscape, often described as resembling the "Wild West" of Ciudad Real. The trail traverses farmlands with olive groves, open holm oak dehesas, scrubland, and pine forests. A significant part of the route follows the Camino Natural del Guadiana, offering views of the Guadiana River.

faqs.question: Are there any notable natural features or viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route offers beautiful natural scenery. You'll pass through the Raña de las Peñas Lisas dehesas, characterized by its holm oak forests. While this specific loop might not directly enter the dramatic Estrecho de las Hoces gorge, the presence of the Guadiana River and its surrounding landscape provides impressive views. Keep an eye out for diverse flora like junipers, strawberry trees, and rockrose.

faqs.question: What wildlife might I encounter on the Puebla de Don Rodrigo and Camino Natural del Guadiana Loop?
faqs.answer: The area is rich in wildlife. Along the riverbanks and in the dehesas, you might spot otters, roe deer, storks, ducks, and kingfishers. The region's ecological value is high, as many sections run through protected areas for birds.

faqs.question: Is the Puebla de Don Rodrigo and Camino Natural del Guadiana Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are welcome on trails in this region. However, always keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through farmlands or areas with livestock, and to protect local wildlife. Ensure you carry enough water for both yourself and your dog, as water sources might be limited.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: Spring (March to May) and Autumn (September to November) are generally the best times. During these seasons, the weather is milder, and the landscape is vibrant. Summers can be very hot, making hiking less comfortable, while winters can be cooler but still pleasant on sunny days.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: The loop starts and ends near Puebla de Don Rodrigo. You should be able to find parking within or on the outskirts of the village, which serves as a convenient starting point for the trail. Look for designated parking areas or suitable roadside spots.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, approximately 50% of this route follows sections of the Camino Natural del Guadiana, a significant long-distance trail that explores the Guadiana River basin. This means you'll be hiking on well-established paths that are part of a larger network.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for this hike?
faqs.answer: For this specific loop, a permit or entrance fee is generally not required. However, some more sensitive or protected areas within the broader region of the Camino Natural del Guadiana might require advance booking or accompaniment by environmental agents. This loop is typically accessible without such requirements.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or shops near the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail starts and ends in Puebla de Don Rodrigo, where you can find basic amenities such as cafes, bars, and small shops to stock up on supplies before or after your hike. It's always a good idea to carry sufficient water and snacks for the duration of the 10.5 km loop.

faqs.question: Is this loop suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: Given its moderate difficulty and 10.5 km distance, this loop might be a bit long for very young children. However, older children who are accustomed to hiking should manage well. The varied terrain and potential for wildlife spotting can make it an engaging experience for families. For shorter, more accessible options in the region, look for trails specifically designed for families, some of which feature panels and walkways over the river.

waypoints.name: Raña de las Peñas Lisas dehesas
